author: doll-agent
content: Partial progress: added PolynomialF32 (proof-of-concept generic-Float lift, power basis only) in a new ferray-polynomial::power_f32 module. Mirrors the f64 Polynomial API: new, coeffs, degree, with_domain, with_window, eval (Horner with domain→window mapping), deriv, integ, add/sub/mul, trim, truncate, fit (internally promotes to f64 for the Vandermonde solve, demotes the result), roots (same upcast pattern), to_f64 / from_f64 bridges. 11 unit tests cover constant/quadratic eval, deriv-then-integ roundtrip, arithmetic, trim/truncate, linear fit, real-roots quadratic, and the f64 bridges. Stays open: the same structural pattern still needs to be applied to the other 5 bases (Chebyshev, Hermite, HermiteE, Laguerre, Legendre) and to Complex<T> coefficients. Each is a mechanical replication of the power_f32 module — ~400 lines per basis. The Poly trait redesign to take T: Element + Float as a generic parameter remains the bigger architectural call (it would unify the 12 forthcoming PolyF32/PolyComplex types under one trait); deferring that until concrete user demand surfaces.
